title: Crostata di pere e cioccolato
subtitle: Italian pear and dark chocolate tart with a crumbly pasta frolla pastry.
excerpt: Crostata di pere e cioccolato is an Italian tart: a crumbly sweet pastry case filled with dark chocolate cream and topped with thinly sliced poached pear. The pastry is pasta frolla, which is richer and more biscuit-like than French pâte sucrée. The chocolate filling is dense and ganache-like rather than a pastry cream.
sourceNotes: Crostata is the standard Italian tart format, found across all regions with both sweet and savoury fillings. Pasta frolla (short pastry) is the national pastry dough, typically flavoured with lemon zest. Pear and chocolate is a classic Italian flavour pairing, appearing in both tarts and cakes across the north. The combination is especially associated with the autumn and winter, when Conference and Comice pears are at their best. This recipe follows the northern Italian tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: The blind-baked tart case can be frozen. The assembled tart does not freeze well.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry case can be blind-baked the day before. The assembled tart keeps in the fridge for two days; the pears will discolour slightly on the surface but the flavour is unaffected.

recipeTools.notes: Heatproof bowl for melting the chocolate.

glossaryTerms.definition: Italian short pastry. Richer than standard shortcrust because it uses icing sugar and egg yolks rather than caster sugar and whole egg. The result is more crumbly and biscuit-like, with a deeper golden colour.

content.text: This tart has three distinct layers. The

base is blind-baked until golden. The chocolate filling is poured in and chilled until set. The poached pears go on top, thinly sliced, fanned out over the surface. Each stage is simple; they just need to happen in order and with enough time for the chocolate to set before the pears go on.

Rub {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and cold {{unsalted-butter}} (the pastry quantity) together until like breadcrumbs. Add {{egg-yolks}} and {{lemon-zest}} and mix to a firm dough. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick, line the tart tin, and trim. Refrigerate for 15 minutes. Line with baking paper, fill with baking beans, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 10 to 12 minutes until pale golden. Cool completely.

Peel and halve {{pear}}. Bring 400ml water, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} to a simmer in a saucepan. Add the pear halves and poach for 8 to 12 minutes until just tender when pierced with a knife. Remove and leave to cool completely, then slice thinly.
Make the chocolate filling and assemble
Heat {{double-cream}} to a simmer and pour over {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl. Stir until smooth. Add the butter portion of {{unsalted-butter}} and stir until melted.
Pour the chocolate filling into the cooled pastry case and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes until set to a firm ganache.
Arrange the sliced pears over the set chocolate, fanning them from the centre or in concentric rings. Serve at room temperature.

attrs.heading: Troubleshooting
attrs.intro: The most common issue is timing the stages.

items.cause: Not chilled for long enough, or cream-to-chocolate ratio too high.
items.fix: Return to the fridge and check again after 30 minutes. A 1:1 ratio of cream to chocolate sets to a firm, sliceable ganache.
Slice and arrange the pears just before serving, or toss the slices briefly in a little of the cooled poaching liquid which contains enough sugar to slow browning.
